meta.title: Wish, Would Rather & It's Time
meta.description: Master 'wish', 'would rather', 'it's time' and other subjunctive-like structures.
meta.description_ko: 'wish', 'would rather', 'it's time' 및 기타 가정법 유사 구조를 학습합니다.

explanation.en: 'Wish + past simple' = desire for something different from the present reality.
explanation.ko: 'wish + 과거 단순' = 현재 현실과 다른 것을 바라는 표현.

questions.prompt: 'I wish I knew the answer.'의 의미는?

explanation.en: 'Wish + past perfect' = regret about something that didn't happen in the past.
explanation.ko: 'wish + 과거완료' = 과거에 일어나지 않은 일에 대한 후회.

questions.prompt: I wish I _____ harder when I was at university. (대학 시절 더 열심히 공부했더라면 좋았을 텐데.)

explanation.en: 'Wish + would' = desire for a change in a current or future situation (often annoyance).
explanation.ko: 'wish + would' = 현재 또는 미래 상황의 변화를 바라는 표현(종종 짜증 표현).

questions.prompt: 'I wish it would stop raining!' — 이 구조는 무엇을 나타내나요?
choices: 미래 또는 현재 상황의 변화 바람

explanation.en: 'Would rather + base verb' (no 'to') expresses preference.
explanation.ko: 'would rather + 동사 원형'('to' 없이)은 선호를 표현합니다.

questions.prompt: She would rather _____ at home than go to the party. (그녀는 파티에 가는 것보다 집에 있는 것을 더 좋아합니다.)

explanation.en: 'Would rather + subject + past simple' = preference about another person's action.
explanation.ko: 'would rather + 주어 + 과거 단순' = 다른 사람의 행동에 대한 선호.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: I'd rather you didn't smoke in here.

explanation.en: 'It's time + subject + past simple' = a suggestion that someone should do something now.
explanation.ko: 'It's time + 주어 + 과거 단순' = 지금 무언가를 해야 한다는 권유.

questions.prompt: 'It's time you went to bed.' — 이 문장에서 'went'를 쓴 이유는?
choices: 'It's time + 주어 + 과거형'은 현재 권유를 나타내기 때문

explanation.en: 'It's high time + subject + past simple' = strong suggestion/criticism that something is overdue.
explanation.ko: 'it's high time + 주어 + 과거 단순' = 무언가가 이미 늦었다는 강한 권유/비판.

questions.prompt: It's high time the government _____ action on climate change. (정부가 기후 변화에 조치를 취할 때가 됐습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Wish + wouldn't' = annoyance about a repeated behaviour.
explanation.ko: 'wish + wouldn't' = 반복적인 행동에 대한 짜증 표현.

questions.prompt: I wish you _____ make so much noise! I'm trying to concentrate. (그렇게 소란스럽게 하지 않았으면 좋겠어요! 집중하려고 노력 중입니다.)

explanation.en: 'Wish + past simple (could)' for present wishes: 'I wish I could speak'.
explanation.ko: 현재 소망에는 'wish + 과거 단순(could)': 'I wish I could speak'.

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'I wish I can speak Japanese fluently.'

explanation.en: Different 'wish' structures for different time references and meanings.
explanation.ko: 다양한 시제 참조와 의미를 위한 다양한 'wish' 구조.

questions.prompt: 'wish' 구조와 의미를 연결하세요.

explanation.en: 'Would rather + base verb' without 'to'.
explanation.ko: 'would rather + 동사 원형'('to' 없이).

questions.prompt: I'd rather _____ by train than fly — I hate airports. (나는 비행기보다 기차로 여행하는 것을 더 좋아합니다 — 공항이 싫어요.)

explanation.en: 'If only + past perfect' = strong regret about a past action.
explanation.ko: 'if only + 과거완료' = 과거 행동에 대한 강한 후회.

questions.prompt: 'If only I hadn't said that!'의 의미는?
choices: 그 말을 하지 않았더라면 좋았을 텐데

explanation.en: 'It's about time + subject + past simple' = similar to 'it's high time'.
explanation.ko: 'It's about time + 주어 + 과거 단순' = 'it's high time'과 유사.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.

explanation.en: 'Would you rather + subject + past simple' = asking for someone's preference.
explanation.ko: 'would you rather + 주어 + 과거 단순' = 상대방의 선호를 묻는 표현.

questions.prompt: Would you rather I _____ the meeting to Thursday? (회의를 목요일로 바꾸는 것이 나으시겠어요?)

explanation.en: In formal/subjunctive contexts, 'were' is used for all persons with 'wish'.
explanation.ko: 격식체/가정법 맥락에서는 'wish'와 함께 모든 인칭에 'were'를 씁니다.

questions.prompt: 'I wish I were taller.' — 왜 'were'를 쓰나요?

explanation.en: 'Would rather + subject + past simple' for preferences about others' actions.
explanation.ko: 다른 사람의 행동에 대한 선호에는 'would rather + 주어 + 과거 단순'.

questions.prompt: She'd rather her children _____ so much time on their phones. (그녀는 아이들이 휴대폰에 그렇게 많은 시간을 쏟지 않았으면 합니다.)

explanation.en: 'Wish + past simple (had)' for current wishes: 'I wish I had more free time.'
explanation.ko: 현재 소망에 'wish + 과거 단순(had)': 'I wish I had more free time.'

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'I wish I have more free time.'

explanation.en: 'If only + past perfect' = strong regret about a past missed opportunity.
explanation.ko: 'if only + 과거완료' = 과거의 놓친 기회에 대한 강한 후회.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: If only I had listened to my parents' advice.

explanation.en: 'Would sooner + base verb' = prefer to do something else (similar to 'would rather').
explanation.ko: 'would sooner + 동사 원형' = 다른 것을 더 선호하다('would rather'와 유사).

questions.prompt: He'd sooner _____ than admit he was wrong. (그는 자신이 틀렸다고 인정하기보다 차라리 사직하겠습니다.)

explanation.en: 'I wish it weren't hot' = desire for cooler weather (current reality is hot).
explanation.ko: 'I wish it weren't hot' = 시원한 날씨를 바라는 것(현재는 더운 상태).

questions.prompt: 'I wish it weren't so hot today.'와 같은 의미는?
choices: I hope it isn't hot today.
I want the weather to be cooler.

explanation.en: 'It's time + subject + past simple' OR 'It's time + to-infinitive' (without subject).
explanation.ko: 'it's time + 주어 + 과거 단순' 또는 'it's time + to 부정사'(주어 없이).

questions.prompt: It's time _____ our attitude towards the environment. (환경에 대한 우리의 태도를 바꿀 때가 됐습니다.)

explanation.en: 'I would rather you didn't + verb' = polite way to say you prefer they don't do something.
explanation.ko: 'I would rather you didn't + 동사' = 상대방이 무언가를 하지 않기를 선호하는 정중한 표현.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: I would rather you didn't come.

explanation.en: Past regret about a career choice: 'wish + past perfect (had chosen)'.
explanation.ko: 직업 선택에 대한 과거 후회: 'wish + 과거완료(had chosen)'.

questions.prompt: 빈칸에 알맞은 것은? 'A: Are you happy with your current job? B: Not really. I wish I _____ a different career.'
